revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Ayatokunaka Shrine (綾戸國中神社) |image = File:Ayatokunaka jinja.JPG |Location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Minami-ku, Kyoto|en|Minami-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154649|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Kyoto}} Kuze Kamikuze-cho 446 |Latitude = 34°57'49.233"N |Longitude = 135°42'55.743"E |Map Code = JP |Map = Japan Kyoto city2 |Label Position = right |Deities = '''Ayato Shrine'''
{{ill|Ōagatashinokami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōagatashinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}}・{{ill|Ōnaohinokami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaohinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Ōnaobi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}}・{{ill|Kaminahinokami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminahinokami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86725467}}
'''Kunikai Shrine'''
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |Rank = Shikinaisha (small) ・ {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Village Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |Founding = |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Ryuzō|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternate Name = |Annual Festival = Second Sunday in May |Rituals = Gion Festival Kuze Komagata Child Priest |prefecture= {{ill|Minami-ku|qid=Q1154649|1=Minami-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.963888888889|N|135.71583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Ayatokunaka Shrine''' (綾戸国中神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Minami-ku, Kyoto|en|Minami-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154649|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Kyoto}}. It was originally two shrines, {{ill|Ayato Shrine|ja|綾戸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayato Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kunikai Shrine|ja|國中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunikai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国中神社|1=Kuninaka Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420409}}, which were merged. The {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}} is a ritual celebrated jointly with {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}. == Deities == ; Ayato Shrine * {{ill|Ōagatashinokami|ja|大綾津日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōagatashinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 禍津日神|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11591203|1=Magatsuhi-no-kami}} (まがつひのかみ) * {{ill|Ōnaohinokami|ja|大直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnaohinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 直毘神|1=Ōnaobi no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581794|1=Ōnaobi no Kami}} (おおなおひのかみ) * {{ill|Kaminahinokami|ja|神直日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaminahinokami|1=Kamuna'obi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q86725467}} (なおびのかみ) ; Kunaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (すさのおみこと) == History == === Ayato Shrine === It is said to have been founded in the 15th year of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} ({{ill|521|en|521|qid=Q31436|lt=521 AD|1=521}}), and was once known as Ōi-sha, serving as the purification deity of the Ōigawa ({{ill|Katsura River|en|Katsura River|qid=Q2909307|lt=Kaguigawa|1=Katsura River}}), but in 965 (Tenryaku 9) it was renamed Ayato-sha. Thereafter, until modern times, it was also referred to as Ōi-sha. It had long been revered as the tutelary deity ({{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}) of Kamikuze. It is one of the classified shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under the entry “Ibarata Shrine, Oto-kun District, Yamashiro Province” or “Ōi Shrine”. The inscription on Ayato Shrine’s nameplate is said to be in the hand of {{ill|Emperor Go-Reizei|en|Emperor Go-Reizei|qid=Q356060|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}.[https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=10&ManageCode=2151 綾戸國中神社 - 京都観光Navi] 2018th year 6月1日　閲覧 === Kunaka Shrine === According to tradition, when the surrounding area was once covered by lake water, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} descended from the heavens and diverted the water, enshrining a carved head of his beloved horse (Tenkō-koma) as a memento. It is one of the classified shrines listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under the entry “Kunaka Shrine, Oto-kun District, Yamashiro Province”. Originally, it had its shrine grounds in the forest of Zao (present-day Zao-dō Kōfuku-ji), and in the medieval period it was known as Gozu Tennō Shrine. It had long been revered as the communal shrine for the entire Kuze district.[http://hitomachideaimon.seesaa.net/article/5422877.html Ayatokunaka Shrine priest Kanafu Sugiura’s remarks on FM797] 2018th year 6月1日　閲覧 === As Ayatokunaka Shrine === However, during the Sengoku period, Kunaka Shrine was relocated to the precincts of Ayato Shrine. Thereafter, two shrine halls stood side by side, but they were destroyed in 1934 (Showa 9) by the {{ill|1934 Muroto typhoon|en|1934 Muroto typhoon|qid=Q4563431|lt=Muroto Typhoon|1=1934 Muroto typhoon}}, and in 1936 (Showa 11) they were rebuilt as a single shrine hall. In 1964 (Showa 39), as the shrine grounds were included in the planned construction area of the {{ill|Tokaido Shinkansen|en|Tokaido Shinkansen|qid=Q660895|lt=Tokaido Shinkansen|1=Tokaido Shinkansen}}, they were relocated to the present site. == Festivals == === Kuze Komagata Child Priest === The Kuze Komagata Child Priest of the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}} is selected from among the parishioners of this shrine. In the divine processions (Shinkō-sai and Kankō-sai) of the Gion Festival, the child priest rides a horse and leads the portable shrine ({{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}) in which {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} is calmed; the wooden carving of a horse’s neck (Komagata) worn on the chest is modeled after the sacred object of Kunaka Shrine.[http://www.mishima-tei.co.jp/news/0005.html Kuze Komagata Child Priest took a break – Mishima-tei] 2018th year 6月1日　閲覧 Normally, within the shrine precincts, child priests armed with long-tachi halberds—and even members of the imperial family—are required to dismount (imperial dismount), but the Kuze Komagata Child Priest, by divine mandate, does not dismount and proceeds directly into the precincts of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}. Furthermore, it is said that the {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Yasaka Shrine is of the nigimitama (harmonious spirit), whereas that of Ayatokunaka Shrine is of the aramitama (rough spirit). == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Minami-ku, Kyoto|en|Minami-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1154649|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Kyoto}} Kuze Kamikuze-cho 446 ; Access * Nearest station: {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|qid=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} – after disembarking at {{ill|Katsuragawa Station (Kyoto)|en|Katsuragawa Station (Kyoto)|qid=Q1071814|lt=Kaguigawa Station|1=Katsuragawa Station (Kyoto)}}, approximately a 6-minute walk ; Surroundings * A 5-minute walk west from the western end of Kuze Bridge; just before the torii gate on the left, there is parking for 2–3 vehicles for visitors. * Across the elevated track of the Shinkansen on the west side, the former site of a pharmacy now serves as the shrine office. == Footnotes == === References === {{Citation Help|qq=}} {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.ayatokunaka.com] {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Shinto Side|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|綾戸国中神社|version=103882278|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Ayato-Kunaka Shrine (Q11607305)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamashiro Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Otokuni District, Kyoto|en|Otokuni District, Kyoto|ja|乙訓郡|ceb|Otokuni-gun|es|Distrito de Otokuni|fa|شهرستان اوتوکونی، کیوتو|fr|District d'Otokuni|id|Distrik Otokuni, Kyōto|it|Distretto di Otokuni|ko|오토쿠니군|pl|Powiat Otokuni|ru|Отокуни|uk|Повіт Отокуні|vi|Otokuni (huyện)|zh-yue|乙訓郡|zh|乙訓郡|lt=Otokuni district|qid=Q376858|1=Otokuni District, Kyoto}} || {{ill|Ayato-Kunaka Shrine|lt=Ayatokunaka Shrine|ja|綾戸国中神社|qid=Q11607305|1=Ayato-Kunaka Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|qid=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.963889|135.715833|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180577 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11607305}}==Notes== [[ja:綾戸国中神社]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:久世 (京都市)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市南区の建築物]] [[Category:京都市南区の歴史]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]]
